children.value: A fully interactive, responsive periodic table web application with 3D visualizations, built with

# Step 1: Navigate to the project folder
cd /Users/jesse/Desktop/Company/Tools/PeriodicTable/HTML/html2

# Step 2: Start the server
python3 server.py

# Step 3: Open your browser
# Browser opens automatically to http://localhost:8000
That’s it! You now have an interactive periodic table with 3D visualizations.
- Visual getting started guide (open in browser)
Interactive grid that responds to clicks
Interactive 3D Bohr models for each element
Rotate, zoom, and explore atomic structures
The periodic table displays all 118 elements in their correct positions
Elements are color-coded by category (alkali metals, halogens, noble gases, etc.)
Click any element to open the modal
See basic information like atomic mass, period, and group
Drag to rotate, scroll to zoom
View in AR on compatible devices
- See what the element looks like physically
Press Escape, click X, or click outside the modal
No frameworks, no build tools, no complex setup!
- Full 18-column grid with side-by-side modal layout

# Node.js
npx http-server .

# PHP
php -S localhost:8000

# Ruby
ruby -run -ehttpd . -p8000
Click on Hydrogen to see a complete example
Try different categories to see the color coding
: Check your internet connection (models load from Google CDN)
: Kill the existing process or modify the port in server.py
: Hard refresh your browser (Ctrl+Shift+R or Cmd+Shift+R)
The application can be deployed to:
3D Models: Loaded on demand from CDN
Everything is set up and ready to use. Just run:
in your browser and start exploring!
Check the browser console (F12) for errors

Built with HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.
No frameworks. No dependencies (except 3D viewer CDN).
Pure web technology.
